Here on this place is story by
itself Here is young woman who
is trying to make man OF her
brother. They have taken up ad-


joining sections. The young man
could not keep straight in town
they come from, Illinois and the
father is a BANKRUPT he seems
|all right now, and they are work
ing out two fine farms.


Here are the farms of two Jew
is hlawyers from Boston-the
the brothers Cohen. The Jew on
the farm is not usual. and from
Boston law to a Montana irrigation
farm is a jump. But they are do
ing fairly well.


Here are two Hollanders on ad


joining sections. They know how
to farm. They are getting on splen-
didiy.
"This is the farm of an Italian
from Chicago. He was, believe,
in the retail fruit business before


he decided to try farming. He


works like a mailer and his pros
poets are excellent.


"a woman has taken up this
claim We call her Mrs. Mack.


She does al her own work, includ-
ing blowing and taking care of the
horses, and in winter she goes to
Billings and tells fortunes.


cover there is Race Horse


Smiths farm. He used to be
sporting man. Having decided to
cut that out, he has become a
farmer and is doing well. He has
some capital"


Two young boys had taken up
one farm. They did not do well,
and spent last winter attending an
agricultural college. On one farm


we found a Kansas City insurance
agent, on another a painter, on an
other barkeeper. Other occupa.
tons represented were butched,
motOrman, boOkkeeper, pickle


maker locomotive engineer, doctor,
preacher and ditch rider. Only here
and there were there any who had
ever farmed before.


"And they are all doing well ?"
I |. asked the government director.


We'll" he replied carefully, you


see for yourself they are all max


ing a living the first and second
year. | don't know what more any


body can ask than that. IL they can


make their payments and live the


first two years they certainly will
be able to the remaining years.


They are learning the business now
and making plenty of mistakes. But
if, in spite of this, they can make


a living and keep up their pay


ments, they are clearly on the high


road to independence. and in the


end to something more than a


competence.


So there you are. Three-quar-
tiers of the Huntley settlers from
the city-butchers and bakers and


candlestick makers-and all max
ing good at farming. On a capital


of $500 they have a home and a


certainty of living. and with ex-
perience comfortable and care


free old age.


In another article will tell how


much it costs and more about 1t.
PANAMA, Oct. 14.-Senor Uurra-


ga, the distinguished Chilean en-


gineer and author of the Atrato
canal project, arrived here today
en- route to Bogota for the purpose


of obtaining a concession from the
| Colombian government.
